questions.question: Briefly explain the difference between an iterative process and an incremental process.
expectedPoints: Iterative refers to refining and improving the product through repeated cycles (revisiting).
Incremental refers to building the product piece by piece, adding features sequentially (adding).
Most modern models (like Agile) combine both approaches.

questions.question: Compare the Iterative/Incremental model with the traditional Waterfall model. Discuss their approaches to changing requirements, risk management, and customer involvement.
expectedPoints: Approach: Waterfall is linear/sequential; Iterative is cyclical.
Changing Requirements: Waterfall is rigid, changes are difficult/costly; Iterative incorporates changes between iterations.
Risk Management: Waterfall discovers risks late during testing; Iterative identifies and mitigates risks early in each cycle.
Customer Involvement: Waterfall involves customer mainly at start/end; Iterative involves customer for feedback at each increment.
Conclusion: Iterative is more flexible and adaptive for complex or uncertain projects.
